content:
# Numbers Chapter 2 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 147-148; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 111-112 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_002_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_002_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## The Command to Encamp by Standards (vv. 1-2)

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

**2** "The sons of Israel shall encamp each man by his own standard, with the signs of the house of their fathers; at a distance, surrounding the tent of meeting, they shall encamp.

---

## The East Camp: Judah (vv. 3-9)

**3** And those who encamp on the east side, toward the sunrise -- the standard of the camp of Judah, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Judah shall be Nahshon son of Amminadab.

**4** And his host and their mustered ones: seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

**5** And those who encamp beside him -- the tribe of Issachar; and the leader of the sons of Issachar shall be Nethanel son of Zuar.

**6** And his host and their mustered ones: fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

**7** The tribe of Zebulun; and the leader of the sons of Zebulun shall be Eliab son of Helon.

**8** And his host and their mustered ones: fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

**9** All the mustered ones of the camp of Judah: one hundred and eighty-six thousand and four hundred, by their hosts. They shall set out first.

---

## The South Camp: Reuben (vv. 10-16)

**10** The standard of the camp of Reuben shall be on the south side, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Reuben shall be Elizur son of Shedeur.

**11** And his host and their mustered ones: forty-six thousand and five hundred.

**12** And those who encamp beside him -- the tribe of Simeon; and the leader of the sons of Simeon shall be Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

**13** And his host and their mustered ones: fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

**14** And the tribe of Gad; and the leader of the sons of Gad shall be Eliasaph son of Reuel.

**15** And his host and their mustered ones: forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.

**16** All the mustered ones of the camp of Reuben: one hundred and fifty-one thousand and four hundred and fifty, by their hosts. And they shall set out second.

---

## The Levites and the Tent of Meeting (v. 17)

**17** And the tent of meeting shall set out, the camp of the Levites, in the midst of the camps. As they encamp, so shall they set out, each man at his station, by their standards.

---

## The West Camp: Ephraim (vv. 18-24)

**18** The standard of the camp of Ephraim, by their hosts, shall be on the west side; and the leader of the sons of Ephraim shall be Elishama son of Ammihud.

**19** And his host and their mustered ones: forty thousand and five hundred.

**20** And beside him -- the tribe of Manasseh; and the leader of the sons of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

**21** And his host and their mustered ones: thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

**22** And the tribe of Benjamin; and the leader of the sons of Benjamin shall be Abidan son of Gideoni.

**23** And his host and their mustered ones: thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

**24** All the mustered ones of the camp of Ephraim: one hundred and eight thousand and one hundred, by their hosts. And they shall set out third.

---

## The North Camp: Dan (vv. 25-31)

**25** The standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Dan shall be Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

**26** And his host and their mustered ones: sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

**27** And those who encamp beside him -- the tribe of Asher; and the leader of the sons of Asher shall be Pagiel son of Ochran.

**28** And his host and their mustered ones: forty-one thousand and five hundred.

**29** And the tribe of Naphtali; and the leader of the sons of Naphtali shall be Ahira son of Enan.

**30** And his host and their mustered ones: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

**31** All the mustered ones of the camp of Dan: one hundred and fifty-seven thousand and six hundred. They shall set out last, by their standards.

---

## The Grand Total and Obedience (vv. 32-34)

**32** These are the mustered ones of the sons of Israel by the houses of their fathers. All the mustered ones of the camps, by their hosts: six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

**33** But the Levites were not mustered among the sons of Israel, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**34** And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses; so they encamped by their standards, and so they set out, each man by his clans, by the house of his fathers.

---

## SOPHIA's Consensus Notes

### Manuscript Confirmation

The Leningrad Codex pages 147-148 (West Semitic Research Project photographs by Bruce E. Zuckerman) present Numbers 2 in three-column format. Page 147 (the first image) continues from Numbers 1 and carries the opening of chapter 2, with YHWH's command for the tribal camp arrangement opening with the directive concerning the degel ("standard"). The text proceeds through the eastern camp of Judah (vv. 3-9), the southern camp of Reuben (vv. 10-16), and the Levitical center position (v. 17). Page 148 (the second image) continues with the western camp of Ephraim (vv. 18-24), the northern camp of Dan (vv. 25-31), and the grand total and obedience formula (vv. 32-34). The Masorah parva annotations are visible in the margins throughout. The Codex Vaticanus (Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana, Vat.gr.1209, pp. 111-112) presents the Greek Arithmoi in three-column uncial. Page 111 carries the end of Numbers 1 and the beginning of Numbers 2, with the transition clearly visible. Page 112 continues through the remainder of the chapter. The LXX follows the MT tribal order precisely; all twelve census figures and the four camp totals match the Hebrew. The grand total of 603,550 is consistent across both textual traditions.

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **"Standard" and "signs" (v. 2):** EMET renders degel as "standard" and otot as "signs"; LOGOS renders "standard" and "ensigns"; PNEUMA renders "standard" and "banners." SOPHIA adopts "standard" for degel (unanimous across all three) and "signs" for otot, following EMET. The Hebrew otot means "signs" -- a broad term for identifying markers. LOGOS's "ensigns" is an elegant distinction from "standard" but introduces a word not directly demanded by the Hebrew. PNEUMA's "banners" collapses the distinction between degel and otot by rendering both with banner-related terms. "Signs" preserves the Hebrew term's breadth -- the same word designates the signs of creation (Genesis 1:14), the signs before Pharaoh (Exodus 4:8-9), and circumcision as a sign (Genesis 17:11). Rendering it transparently as "signs" allows these intertextual connections to stand.

2. **"At a distance, surrounding" (v. 2):** EMET renders minneged saviv as "at a distance, surrounding"; LOGOS renders "at a distance around"; PNEUMA renders "at a distance, facing the tent of meeting on every side." SOPHIA adopts "at a distance, surrounding" -- a close rendering of the two Hebrew words. The preposition minneged ("from opposite/at a distance") establishes the spatial buffer between the tribes and the sacred center, while saviv ("surrounding/all around") describes the encircling formation. PNEUMA's expansion ("facing... on every side") adds interpretive material; the Hebrew does not specify that the tribes face the tent, only that they surround it at a distance. The LXX's enantios kyklo ("opposite in a circle") confirms the dual sense of distance and encirclement.

3. **"Mustered ones" (throughout):** Continuing the established SOPHIA rendering from Numbers 1, pequdim is rendered "mustered ones" throughout, not "numbered ones" (LOGOS) or "counted" (PNEUMA). The covenantal-military range of paqad was established in the Numbers 1 consensus notes and applies uniformly here. The census figures are identical across all three translators and both manuscript traditions.

4. **"Hosts" vs. "divisions" (throughout):** EMET and LOGOS render tsiv'otam as "their hosts"; PNEUMA renders "their divisions." SOPHIA follows the EMET/LOGOS consensus with "their hosts," consistent with the Numbers 1 rendering. The term tsava' denotes the organized assembly under divine command, and "host" preserves the theological overtone of YHWH as commander of the heavenly and earthly hosts.

5. **"East side, toward the sunrise" (v. 3):** EMET renders qedmah mizrakhah as "eastward, toward the sunrise"; LOGOS renders "on the east side toward the sunrise"; PNEUMA renders "on the east side, toward the sunrise." SOPHIA adopts "on the east side, toward the sunrise" following the LOGOS/PNEUMA majority, which reads naturally while preserving the Hebrew doublet. The double expression (qedmah + mizrakhah) is emphatic, and both elements are retained.

6. **"South side" (v. 10):** EMET renders teimanah as "southward"; LOGOS renders "on the south side"; PNEUMA renders "on the south side." SOPHIA follows "on the south side" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority) for consistency with the other directional phrases in the chapter ("on the east side," "on the west side," "on the north side"). The Hebrew teimanah is a directional he-locale form meaning "toward the south."

7. **"West side" (v. 18):** EMET renders yammah as "seaward" with a note explaining the westward sense; LOGOS and PNEUMA render "on the west side." SOPHIA adopts "on the west side" because the English reader cannot infer "west" from "seaward" without the ancient Near Eastern geographic orientation. The Hebrew idiom is geographically bound to the Mediterranean's position relative to Israel; the translation must convey the directional meaning, not the geographic mechanism.

8. **"At his station" (v. 17):** EMET renders al-yado as "at his place" with a literal note ("upon his hand"); LOGOS renders "in his place"; PNEUMA renders "in position." SOPHIA renders "at his station" -- a rendering that preserves the positional meaning of the idiom while using "station" (rather than the more generic "place") to convey the military-organizational context. Each man holds his assigned station in the marching order. The LXX's kata taxin ("according to order") confirms the military-positional sense.

9. **"Set out" (vv. 9, 16, 17, 24, 31):** All three translators use "set out" for yissa'u (Qal imperfect of nasa'). Full consensus. This is consistent with the SOPHIA rendering established in Numbers 1:51 and Exodus 40:36-37.

10. **"Reuel" (v. 14):** All three translators read Re'u'el in v. 14, following the Leningrad Codex's resh reading. In Numbers 1:14, the same figure was named "Deuel" (De'u'el), following the LC's dalet reading there. All three translators note the well-known dalet/resh graphic confusion. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads Ragouēl (= Reuel) consistently. SOPHIA follows the MT reading in each verse: Deuel in 1:14, Reuel in 2:14.

11. **"So they encamped...and so they set out" (v. 34):** EMET renders ken khanu...ve-khen nasa'u as "so they encamped...and so they set out"; LOGOS renders identically; PNEUMA renders "that is how they camped...and that is how they set out." SOPHIA follows the EMET/LOGOS rendering, preserving the double ken ("so/thus") construction that affirms total obedience. The paired "so...and so" mirrors the Hebrew's rhetorical parallelism more tightly than PNEUMA's "that is how...and that is how."

### Camp Summary Table

| Position | Standard | Tribes | Leader (first tribe) | Total |
|----------|----------|--------|---------------------|-------|
| East (qedmah) | Judah | Judah, Issachar, Zebulun | Nahshon son of Amminadab | 186,400 |
| South (teimanah) | Reuben | Reuben, Simeon, Gad | Elizur son of Shedeur | 151,450 |
| Center | Levites + Tent of Meeting | -- | -- | (not counted) |
| West (yammah) | Ephraim | Ephraim, Manasseh, Benjamin | Elishama son of Ammihud | 108,100 |
| North (tsafonah) | Dan | Dan, Asher, Naphtali | Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai | 157,600 |
| **Grand Total** | | | | **603,550** |

### Consistent Term Renderings (Numbers Series)

| Hebrew | SOPHIA Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| benei Yisra'el | sons of Israel | Binding convention |
| YHWH | YHWH | Transliterated; binding convention |
| degel | standard | Tribal military ensign |
| otot | signs | Identifying markers of ancestral houses |
| paqad / pequdim | muster / mustered ones | Covenantal-military range of paqad |
| tsava' / tsiv'otam | host / their hosts | Military assembly under divine command |
| matteh | tribe | Administrative unit |
| nasi' | leader | Tribal chieftain; from nasa' ("to lift up") |
| makhaneh / makhanot | camp / camps | Tribal-division encampment |
| ohel mo'ed | tent of meeting | The place of divine-human encounter |
| nasa' / yissa'u | set out | Decamping; pulling up stakes |
| mishpakhah | clan | Kinship unit between tribe and father's house |
| beit av / beit avot | house of his/their fathers | Patrilineal household |

--- SOPHIA
